faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the platform

items.question: What is Serene?
items.answer: Serene is a quiet place for people living with anxiety. Here you'll find Jiva — our empathetic AI psychologist — a supportive community, proven techniques, and crisis support. Built by people who know these feelings from the inside, so you can simply be yourself and feel that you're not alone.

items.question: Who is Jiva?
items.answer: Jiva is our AI psychologist. Warm, empathetic, deeply human. Under the hood — Claude Sonnet 4.6 from Anthropic, one of the strongest models in the world for conversations about feelings. She thinks before answering, remembers your story (on Premium), gently supports you in a crisis, and hears what's between the lines. Jiva is not a doctor and doesn't give diagnoses, but with her you're never alone.

items.question: What are «Small Joys»?
items.answer: It's a daily gratitude ritual on the home screen. One post a day — about something good that warmed you today: coffee, a hug, a sunbeam in the window. No likes-races, no algorithms — just living sparks of light from real people. Regularly noticing the good lowers anxiety and trains your brain to see the light — a core CBT technique.

items.question: What's available for free?
items.answer: Free includes: the «Small Joys» feed, navigator through anxiety, crisis support with breathing and hotlines, mood diary and SMER entries, and drawing in art therapy. Plus an intro to Jiva: 3 messages + 1 drawing analysis — just to feel who she is.

items.question: What does Premium offer?
items.answer: Anchor unlocks Jiva beyond the intro: 10 deep conversations a day + 5 extra on hard days (3 times a month). Soul History — Jiva remembers your experiences, names and details. Art therapy — 3 drawing analyses a day. For those who want to go deeper. Price: $5 per month.

items.question: Can I cancel my subscription?
items.answer: Yes, anytime. One button in settings. Premium access remains until the end of the paid period — no losses.

creatorLetter.title: Letter from the Creator
creatorLetter.intro: This is the creator of Serene.
creatorLetter.body:
For the past week, you've had access to all Premium features. I hope it helped.

I'm building this project with my own money, and running AI features is expensive (servers, neural networks). I can't afford to pay for it for everyone indefinitely.

So now you have a choice:
creatorLetter.thanks: The choice is yours. Either way — thank you for being here.

Yes, anytime. Access continues until the end of the paid period.
Unlike regular chatbots, this neural network thinks before answering. It builds logical chains, analyzes hidden causes of anxiety, and finds connections you might miss.
No. Serene is a digital support space, not a medical service. For serious conditions, consult specialists.

steps: Stop. Try 4-7-8 breathing — inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 7, exhale for 8.
Find 5 objects around you that you can see. This brings your mind back to reality.
Write down a thought in your anxiety journal: what you feel, what helped.
Remove bright light, enable 'night mode' on your phone.
Do the 5-4-3-2-1 exercise to relax your body.
Try writing down a thought or fear — this reduces tension.
Text someone you trust that you're feeling anxious.
Prepare a short message: 'Things are tough right now, just be here with me'.
If emotions are overwhelming — go to crisis mode, there are hotlines there.

answer: Try saying out loud: 'This is a panic attack, it's safe, it will pass in 10 minutes'.
Ground yourself — feel your feet, describe three objects around, name smells.
Ask openly: 'Do you want me to just listen?'.
Don't give quick advice unless the person asks.
Remind them that help is available: crisis lines, therapist, our community.
If anxiety interferes with sleep, work or daily communication.
If thoughts of self-harm appear or interest in life disappears.
If people around say you're 'unbearably nervous' — that's also a sign.
Don't stop taking it abruptly — call your doctor or the clinic where you're being treated.
If one pill remains — divide it to extend the effect and have time to contact a specialist.
Ask someone close to help organize an appointment or medication delivery.

steps: Name 5 things you can SEE around you
Name 4 things you can TOUCH
Name 3 sounds you can HEAR
Name 2 smells you can SENSE
Name 1 taste you can FEEL
Choose a category (e.g., flowers, cities, names)
Start listing objects from that category in alphabetical order
Focus on each word, saying it slowly
Continue until you feel calmer
Choose any object near you
Study it in detail: color, shape, texture
Describe it out loud or to yourself in as much detail as possible
Focus on physical sensations when touching it

header.title: Specialist directory
header.subtitle: Find a verified psychologist or therapist

empty.suggestion: Try adjusting the filters or come back soon — we add new specialists regularly.

booking.dialogDescription: Pick a time slot that feels comfortable. We'll send a reminder 2 hours before the session.
booking.reminder: We'll send an in-app and email reminder. If plans change, please let the specialist know in advance.
booking.noSlots: No free slots yet. Try another specialist or check back later.

nextSteps.title: Next gentle steps
nextSteps.calendar: Add the session to your calendar and double-check the time zone.
nextSteps.prepare: Prepare a calm space and write down what you want to explore together.
nextSteps.contact: Need to reschedule? Let the specialist know in advance so the slot opens for others.
nextSteps.support: If you need help, text support — we'll help you find another time or specialist.

artTherapy.title: Art Therapy
artTherapy.subtitle: Express emotions
artTherapy.disclaimer: This is a creative interpretation, not a medical diagnosis

smer.title: SMER Diary
smer.subtitle: Track the connection between thoughts and emotions
smer.explanation: SMER is a technique from cognitive behavioral therapy. It helps understand the connection between Situation, Thoughts (Mind), Emotions, and Reaction.
smer.situationHint: What happened? Where were you? Who was there?
smer.thoughtsHint: What thoughts came to mind at that moment?
smer.emotionsHint: Select emotions and rate their intensity (0-10)
smer.reactionHint: What did you do in this situation?
smer.alternativeHint: How could you have reacted differently?
smer.alternativePlaceholder: What could you have done differently?
smer.noEntries: No SMER entries yet. Create your first one!
smer.deleteConfirmDesc: This action cannot be undone. The entry will be permanently deleted.

discussWarning.title: Discuss with Jiva
discussWarning.description: This will use 1 of your 10 daily messages. AI will analyze your entry and help you understand your thoughts.

smer.archiveLocked: Full SMER archive available with Anchor

navigator.title: Navigator

seo.title: Navigator — Guide to Anxiety
seo.description: Verified information about anxiety, coping techniques and support resources

navigator.greeting: We're here to help you understand anxiety. This is verified information from reliable sources. Choose where to start 💙
navigator.disclaimer: We don't diagnose — if anxiety significantly interferes with your life, please consult a specialist.

recommend.title: We recommend
recommend.description: Start with 'What is Anxiety' — understanding how anxiety works gives you power to manage it

professional.title: Professional Help
professional.subtitle: When to see a specialist, treatments

intro.title: What are anxiety disorders?
intro.description: Anxiety is a natural emotion that helps respond to dangers. But when it becomes constant, uncontrollable and interferes with life, it may be an anxiety disorder.

anxiety.aiNote: Remember: anxiety is not a character flaw. It's a signal that you need to take care of yourself. You're not alone.
anxiety.bottomNote: Anxiety is not weakness, but a signal that you need to take care of yourself.

intro.title: Coping techniques
intro.description: These evidence-based methods help in the moment. Start with simple ones.

techniques.aiNote: Try one technique right now. Even 2 minutes of breathing can change your state.

hotlines.title: Crisis Hotlines
hotlines.note: If you're abroad — find local line at who.int

resources.bottomNote: You'll get through this. We're with you.

motivational.advanced: You've mastered several techniques — that's real strength! Keep practicing.
motivational.beginner: You've started your journey! Each technique is a step toward peace. I believe in you 💙
